title: Passing Students
description: You are given three tables: departments, students, and grades. Find names of students who passed (score >= 60) in at least 2 courses, ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
departments(id, name)
students(id, name, dept_id)
grades(id, student_id, course, score)
outputFormat: One student name per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Use JOIN and GROUP BY. Filter WHERE score >= 60. Use HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT s.name FROM students s JOIN grades g ON s.id = g.student_id WHERE g.score >= 60 GROUP BY s.id, s.name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY s.name;
hint: Join students with grades, filter score >= 60, GROUP BY student, HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER, name TEXT); CREATE TABLE students (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE grades (id INTEGER, student_id INTEGER, course TEXT, score INTEGER); INSERT INTO departments VALUES (1,'CS'),(2,'Math'); INSERT INTO students VALUES (1,'Alice',1),(2,'Bob',1),(3,'Carol',2); INSERT INTO grades VALUES (1,1,'Algo',75),(2,1,'DB',80),(3,2,'Algo',55),(4,2,'DB',65),(5,3,'Calc',90),(6,3,'Stats',45);

CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER, name TEXT); CREATE TABLE students (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE grades (id INTEGER, student_id INTEGER, course TEXT, score INTEGER); INSERT INTO departments VALUES (1,'Physics'),(2,'Biology'); INSERT INTO students VALUES (1,'Dan',1),(2,'Eve',1),(3,'Frank',2),(4,'Grace',2); INSERT INTO grades VALUES (1,1,'Mech',70),(2,1,'Thermo',65),(3,2,'Optics',90),(4,2,'EM',50),(5,3,'Cell',60),(6,3,'Genetics',72),(7,4,'Cell',45),(8,4,'Ecology',55);
